title: Fusion Energy Race Update — Apr 21, 2026
summary: The UK unveiled its National Fusion Energy Strategy on April 14 with £1.3 billion in government investment and ILIOS Consortium awarded the £200M STEP construction partner contract, cementing the West Burton facility's 2040 target. ARPA-E announced a $135 million fusion commitment on April 8 — the agency's largest ever — while the French nuclear authority (ASNR) granted ITER a key regulatory exemption for the vacuum vessel design. Private sector investment shows signs of stress: the rolling 12-month fundraising rate fell to ~$1.6B (from a $2.64B mid-2025 peak), with General Fusion pursuing a reverse merger and TAE Technologies completing a multi-state siting tour after its TMTG financing agreement. SHINE Technologies secured a $263M conditional DOE loan for its Chrysalis fusion-based medical isotope facility in Janesville, Wisconsin.

title: Fusion Energy Race Update — Sun Apr 19, 2026
summary: Private Fusion Investment Slows to $1.6B Rolling Annual Rate Amid Industry Stress.

title: Fusion Energy Race Update — Tue Apr 14, 2026
summary: France Grants ITER Vacuum Vessel Key Regulatory Exemption from Pressure Rules. On April 14, 2026, the UK government unveiled its National Fusion Energy Strategy, backed by £1.3 billion in government investment, at Fusion Fest 2026 in London.

title: Fusion Energy Race Update — Thu Apr 9, 2026
summary: SHINE Receives $263M DOE Conditional Loan for Chrysalis Fusion Isotope Facility.

title: Fusion Energy Race Update — Wed Apr 8, 2026
summary: ARPA-E Commits $135 Million to Fusion — Agency's Largest Fusion Investment.

title: Fusion Energy Race Update — Tue Apr 7, 2026
summary: TAE Technologies Completes Multi-State Site Tour for First Fusion Power Plant.
